id: XQL-FUNC-318-dedup-keep-first
title: dedup <field> — collapse to one row per distinct value

---
# dedup <field> — collapse to one row per distinct value

`| dedup <field>` keeps the FIRST row seen for each distinct value of `<field>` and drops the rest (verified: 10 rows across 3 hosts → 3 rows, one per host, keeping the first occurrence of each). Combine with a preceding `| sort` to control WHICH row survives — e.g. `sort desc _time | dedup endpoint` keeps each endpoint's most-recent event. Distinct from `comp ... by` (which aggregates); `dedup` preserves the surviving row's full columns.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name != null
| sort desc _time
| dedup actor_process_image_name
| fields _time, actor_process_image_name, actor_process_command_line
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
